subject: [PATCH v2 4/4] mm: page_alloc: fix non-movable reclaim storm in defrag_mode

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that if patch 3/4 is not included, stable maintainers may inadvertently break the build by applying patches 3/4 and 4/4 separately without also including patch 2/4, which is a hard functional requirement for 4/4

Reviewer noted that the patch series may not apply cleanly or build correctly if patches 2 and 3 are removed, potentially causing issues during automated backporting
reviews.raw_body:
So that's the tricky part then. If it's a hard functional requirement for
4/4 and yet leaving it out results in no conflicts when applying 3/4 and 4/4
(as it indeed seems so, based on my local test), then it's quite possible
stable folks will figure out themselves they need 3/4 as a stable-dep-off:
4/4 but won't backport 2/4, unless you act quickly enough on their mails.

---

The way I see if often happen is that Greg sends a mail that it bounced and
soon after Sasha replies with fixed up version/series. Which could very
easily be 3/4 + 4/4 without 2/4.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton expressed frustration with the patch series and requested that Fixes tags be added to all four patches, specifically referencing a previous commit e3aa7df331bc.
reviews.raw_body:
My brain hurts.  I'll include

Fixes: e3aa7df331bc ("mm: page_alloc: defrag_mode")
Cc: <stable@vger.kernel.org>

in all four patches.

2026-07-23.patch_summary: This patch fixes a reclaim storm issue in defrag_mode by adding a required dependency on another patch (3/4). The fix ensures that compaction can make sufficient progress on non-movable requests, which is a hard functional requirement. Without this patch, the automated backport process may fail if the dependency is lost.

reviews.author: Greg Kroah-Hartman
reviews.summary: Reviewer requested that all patches be tagged to ensure a smooth backport process, citing potential struggles and time delays if the automated backport fails due to missing dependencies.
reviews.raw_body:
Thank you!  Don't make us "figure it out" as that way lies struggles and
time delays and the huge chance I get it wrong.  Tagging them all
ensures none of this happens and it all "just works".

thanks,

greg k-h

2026-07-24.patch_summary: This patch fixes a reclaim storm issue in defrag_mode by adding a required dependency on another patch (3/4). The fix ensures that compaction can make sufficient progress on non-movable requests, which is a hard functional requirement. Without this patch, the automated backport process may fail if the dependency is lost.
